name: Procedural Tube Path
description: A glossy tube swept along a noise-driven 3D curve, lit with a rim light and slowly drifting in 3D. Real generative geometry (Line -> Noise -> Resample -> Sweep), animated cheaply by orbiting the view instead of recooking the mesh -- with an optional in-place curve morph.

network_plan.name: line_backbone
network_plan.role: Straight 40-point line that becomes the raw spine before noise bends it into a flowing path.

network_plan.name: noise_curve
network_plan.role: Bends the straight line into an organic 3D path by displacing each point's position. Low-frequency Sparse noise keeps the curve smooth and flowing, not jagged. The static default (no time on tz) means this cooks once and caches; raising Morphspeed binds tz to absTime to make the path deform IN PLACE.

network_plan.name: resample_smooth
network_plan.role: Re-spaces the displaced points into even-length segments along the arc. This both smooths the backbone (removes the original even-Z spacing kinks) and gives the Sweep a uniform tube wall. Measure=Along Arc for accuracy on the curved spline.

network_plan.name: circle_section
network_plan.role: The closed cross-section profile swept along the backbone to form the tube wall. Drives tube smoothness via divs (radial resolution). Tube thickness is the cross-section Scale set on the Sweep, not the radius here, so radx/rady stay at 1.

network_plan.name: sweep_tube
network_plan.role: THE core technique. Input 0 = cross-section (circle), Input 1 = backbone (resampled curve). Places a scaled copy of the circle perpendicular to the curve at every backbone point, skinning a tube. Scale param sets tube radius; computes the surface and normals for lighting.

network_plan.name: geo_tube
network_plan.role: Hosts the swept tube for rendering and carries the CHEAP animation: a constant rotation on ry/rz driven by absTime so the lit tube turns and the specular highlight sweeps across it -- a matrix multiply, no SOP recook. Material assigned via the Render/Material page.

network_plan.name: phong_tube
network_plan.role: Glossy lit material. Tight specular + a colored rim light give the 'how is that one mesh so dimensional' look as the geo rotates. Diffuse/rim colors exposed as the palette controls. Lives directly inside the COMP, beside geo_tube which it shades.

network_plan.name: cam
network_plan.role: Perspective camera looking at the tube. Slow cheap orbit: tx/tz driven by absTime via sin/cos so the camera circles the geometry. Placed near render/light per layout rules.

network_plan.name: light_key
network_plan.role: Key light for the diffuse + specular sweep. Warm-neutral, positioned high and to one side so the tube's curvature reads.

network_plan.name: render_tube
network_plan.role: Renders geo_tube with cam and light_key. Bounded resolution 1280x720, antialiasing on for clean tube edges, transparent-friendly dark bg so the rim light pops.

network_plan.name: blur_bloom
network_plan.role: Soft, bounded blur of the render -- the bright pass of a one-op bloom. Cheap at 1280px, no feedback so no runaway.

network_plan.name: comp_bloom
network_plan.role: Adds the blurred bright pass back over the sharp render (screen/add) for a controlled glow on the highlights. Glow=0 leaves the render untouched because the blur of a clamped scene contributes little, scaled by the param.

network_plan.name: level_grade
network_plan.role: Final grade: slight gamma/contrast lift so the tube and background separate cleanly. Cheap per-pixel.

network_plan.name: out1
network_plan.role: Contract-fixed terminal / capture target (manifest output_op). Clean single output at 1280x720.

exposed_params.name: Curveamp
exposed_params.label: Curve Amount
exposed_params.drives: noise_curve.amp -- how far the noise bends the spine off the straight line. 0 = nearly straight rod, high = wild looping path.

exposed_params.name: Curvescale
exposed_params.label: Curve Scale
exposed_params.drives: noise_curve.period -- spatial wavelength of the noise. Low = tight knotty wiggles, high = long lazy sweeps.

exposed_params.name: Tuberadius
exposed_params.label: Tube Radius
exposed_params.drives: sweep_tube.scale -- thickness of the swept tube. Thin = wire/noodle, thick = fat pipe.

exposed_params.name: Tuberes
exposed_params.label: Tube Resolution
exposed_params.drives: circle_section.divs -- radial facet count of the cross-section. 3 = triangular prism, high = perfectly round tube.

exposed_params.name: Twist
exposed_params.label: Twist
exposed_params.drives: sweep_tube.twist -- rotates the cross-section along the backbone; with a faceted Tuberes it reads as a candy-cane spiral on the surface.

exposed_params.name: Spinspeed
exposed_params.label: Drift Speed
exposed_params.drives: geo_tube.rx/ry rotation AND cam orbit -- the CHEAP animation. Scales the whole slow-drift motion; 0 freezes the view, geometry never recooks.

exposed_params.name: Morphspeed
exposed_params.label: Morph Speed
exposed_params.drives: noise_curve.tz = absTime*Morphspeed -- the OPTIONAL in-place curve deformation. 0 (default) keeps the SOP chain static/cached; raise it to make the path slowly reshape (this makes the SOPs recook every frame, so keep it low).

exposed_params.name: Glow
exposed_params.label: Glow
exposed_params.drives: blur_bloom.size and the strength of the bloom add -- soft halo around the lit highlights and rim. 0 = crisp render, high = dreamy.

prompt.one_liner: A glossy tube swept along a noise-bent 3D curve, lit with a rim light and slowly drifting -- real generative geometry, animated by orbiting the view instead of recooking the mesh.
prompt.evolve: Push Curveamp up and Curvescale down for a knotted tangle, or the reverse for a single lazy ribbon. Crank Twist with a low Tuberes (3-6) for a faceted candy-cane spiral. Set Morphspeed above 0 to make the path slowly reshape in place (it recooks the SOPs -- keep it under ~0.3). Try a near-black background with a saturated Rim color for neon-pipe energy, or warm diffuse + soft Glow for blown-glass. Swap the Sweep cross-section circle for a star or square SOP to change the tube profile entirely. For a forest of tubes, instance geo_tube across a grid with per-instance Seed.

build_notes:
COOK DEMAND: The chain only animates when out1 is demanded (viewer, capture, or warmup bake). In the sandbox add a temp Execute DAT in /specimen_lab (OUTSIDE the COMP) with onFrameStart cooking out1; it never exports. Verify motion with two captures separated by real wall-clock seconds (background sleep), not one forced frame -- a single capture proves nothing.

CHEAP-MOTION ARCHITECTURE (the whole point): the SOP geometry (line_backbone -> noise_curve -> resample_smooth -> sweep_tube) is STATIC and cooks ONCE at the default Morphspeed=0, because noise_curve.tz = absTime*0 = constant. All visible motion is geo_tube's rotation expressions + the cam orbit -- pure matrix transforms, no SOP recook. Confirm sweep_tube.cookedThisFrame == False while the view animates and render_tube.cookedThisFrame == True. Morphspeed > 0 deliberately breaks this (binds tz to time -> SOPs recook every frame); that is the trade the param documents, so warn users and keep its range capped low.

SWEEP WIRING IS ORDER-SENSITIVE: Sweep input 0 = cross-section (circle_section), input 1 = backbone (resample_smooth). Reversed, you get a tube-shaped backbone swept along a tiny circle -- garbage. Use newg=1 so the swept surface gets a clean group; cap='none' leaves the tube open-ended (set to a rounded/flat cap if a closed pill is wanted). Resample BEFORE the sweep, not after -- resampling a tube mesh is meaningless; resampling the curve is what makes the tube wall uniform.

PERFORMANCE: all bounded. Render 1280x720, 8x AA. ~40 backbone points x Tuberes(18) cross-section = a few thousand polys -- trivial CPU/GPU. The bloom is a single Blur + Composite-add (no feedback loop), cheap at 720p. No 4K, no feedback, no unbounded anything. Gate with get_project_performance before/after; the only regression risk is a user setting Tuberes near 48 AND Morphspeed high (SOPs recook a denser mesh every frame) -- still small, but note it. Keep light_key as ONE light; the rim light is a MAT feature (rimlight0*), not a second Light COMP, so it costs nothing extra.

MATERIAL PROXIMITY: phong_tube sits directly inside the COMP beside geo_tube (the geo it shades) and is assigned via geo_tube.par.material='./phong_tube'. cam and light_key sit near render_tube. Param expressions on child ops reach the COMP via parent().par.X (verify parent().path == the specimen COMP path before trusting the depth).

TDN VALUE-ROUNDTRIP TRAP: exposed params export their defaults but NOT current values; a fresh import recreates them at 0/min, so Curveamp/Tuberadius/etc. land inert and the tube collapses to a flat zero-amplitude rod. After ImportNetwork, do a one-time revert-to-default (set par.val = par.default for all 8) or the specimen imports broken. This is a known collection-wide issue -- verify it in the re-import check.

DOCKED DATS: no GLSL here, but phongMAT and the render chain dock nothing problematic; still run get_network_layout after building to confirm nothing scattered. Annotate per section: Backbone Curve (line/noise/resample), Tube Sweep (circle/sweep), Render Scene (geo/mat/cam/light/render), Look (blur/composite/level/out1) -- >=400 units between boxes.

issues: BUG (breaks palette): Phong MAT diffuse color tokens are wrong. Spec uses diffuser/diffuseg/diffuseb; the real TD 2025 tokens are diffr/diffg/diffb (verified docs.derivative.ca/Phong_MAT). The diffuse expressions land on nonexistent params and are dropped on import - tube renders with default diffuse, not the exposed color.

BUG (missing param): The phong_tube material references =parent().par.Colorr/Colorg/Colorb, but exposed_params never defines Colorr/Colorg/Colorb (only Rimr/Rimg/Rimb exist). The diffuse-color control the description promises does not exist. Either add Colorr/g/b to exposed_params or repoint the MAT.

BUG (no spine bend): Noise SOP has no 'operation' parameter. Spec sets operation:pos; the correct token is 'attribute' (value 'pos' is right). With the wrong token dropped, the Noise SOP does not displace position and the backbone may stay a near-straight rod - defeating the whole 'noise-bent curve' premise.

BUG (invalid par): Sweep SOP has no 'cap' parameter (capping is a separate Cap SOP). cap:'none' is invalid and dropped. build_notes claiming cap='none' leaves the tube open-ended is false - the Sweep has no cap control at all.

BUG (invalid menu value): Render TOP antialias:'8x' is a label, not a settable value. Valid internal values are aa1/aa2/aa4/aa8mid/aa8high/aa16low/aa16mid/aa16high/aa32 (verified docs.derivative.ca/Render_TOP). '8x' would be rejected/dropped, falling back to no AA.
BUG (wrong token): Render TOP background alpha token is bgcolora, not bgalpha. bgalpha:1 is dropped; the dark bg's alpha defaults instead of the intended 1.0.

CONCERN (possible black/flat lighting): TD docs do not confirm the Sweep SOP outputs point/vertex normals. Without normals, the Phong diffuse+specular+rim look - the entire 'how is one mesh so dimensional' hook - renders flat or wrong. Untested in spec; must be verified empirically and likely needs a normal-computing step (Facet SOP) between sweep_tube and geo_tube.

MINOR: Line SOP point A/B use array shorthand (pa:[..]/pb:[..]). The real tokens are component-split: pax/pay/paz, pbx/pby/pbz. Whether the TDN importer expands the array form is importer-dependent; component-token form is the safe, verified shape.
critique.fixes: "1) Phong diffuse: change diffuser/diffuseg/diffuseb -> diffr/diffg/diffb. 2) Add exposed params Colorr/Colorg/Colorb (Float, default e.g. 0.2/0.5/0.9, range 0-1, startSection) so the diffuse expressions resolve - OR point diffr/diffg/diffb at existing params. 3) Noise SOP: change 'operation':'pos' -> 'attribute':'pos'. 4) Remove the invalid Sweep 'cap':'none'; if a closed pill is wanted, append a Cap SOP after sweep_tube (cap both ends) and update build_notes - do not claim Sweep has a cap param. 5) Render antialias: change '8x' -> 'aa8high' (or 'aa8mid'). 6) Render bgalpha:1 -> bgcolora:1. 7) Insert a Facet SOP (computenormals=1, or post=1) between sweep_tube and geo_tube to guarantee surface normals for Phong lighting; verify the lit/specular/rim look in a real capture (two captures seconds apart) before shipping - do not declare done on an unlit or flat frame. 8) Optional but safer: express Line points as pax/pay/paz/pbx/pby/pbz component tokens instead of pa/pb arrays. After all edits, re-import via import_network(clear_first=True), do the documented revert-to-default on all exposed params, then capture out1 to confirm a lit, bent, glossy tube (not a flat rod or black frame)."
